title: Heatwave grips Tamil Nadu as IMD issues alert for 19 districts; Madurai records 42.5 degrees celsius
search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo OR "hot loo winds") Tamil Nadu
full_text:
2 Min Read
The India Meteorological Department (IMD) has issued a heatwave warning for 19 districts of Tamil Nadu, saying unusually high temperatures are likely to continue across parts of the state till Friday.
The districts under the heatwave warning on Thursday are Madurai, Vellore, Tirupattur, Ranipet, Kallakurichi, Tiruvannamalai, Kancheepuram, Krishnagiri, Dharmapuri, Salem, Namakkal, Karur, Erode, Tiruchy, Ariyalur, Perambalur, Sivaganga, Virudhunagar and Dindigul.
Madurai Airport recorded 42.5 degrees Celsius on Tuesday. The temperature was 5.5 degrees Celsius above normal and higher than the station's previous July record set in 2017. The weather office said Madurai, Virudhunagar and Dindigul are likely to continue experiencing heatwave conditions till Friday.
VR Durai, head of the Regional Weather Forecasting Centre, said, "During this weekend, weather models are picking up light rainfall activity, which would bring some relief from the extreme heat."

The heat has also reached Tamil Nadu's hill stations. Udhagamandalam (Ooty) recorded 24.8 degrees Celsius on Tuesday, which was 7.4 degrees Celsius above normal and higher than its previous July record of 24.4 degrees Celsius. Coonoor recorded 26.9 degrees Celsius, nearly 5 degrees Celsius above normal.
Kodaikanal also witnessed record heat, with the temperature reaching 24.2 degrees Celsius, 6.3 degrees Celsius above normal. This surpassed its previous July record of 23.9 degrees Celsius, which was set in 1988. In Chennai, temperatures remained high as well.

Tiruchy, Meenambakkam and Cuddalore recorded 39.4 degrees Celsius, while Palayamkottai and Chennai's Nungambakkam weather station registered 38.9 degrees Celsius. Temperatures also crossed 38.6 degrees Celsius in Nagapattinam, Erode, Karur, Parangipettai and Namakkal.
Weather officials said the prolonged hot spell is mainly due to weak southwest monsoon activity. According to the weather department, hot and humid conditions are likely to continue across Tamil Nadu until at least July 16.

According to the Regional Meteorological Centre (RMC) in Chennai, weak southwest monsoon activity has led to the prolonged spell of hot and humid weather. The weather pattern is expected to continue as there are no signs of a strong revival of the monsoon until the end of the month.
